intro: Auriculardisia capitellata, also known as the "Golden Waxy Leaf Plant", is a species of plant in the family Lauraceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Auriculardisia capitellata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
